items.title: Anwar urges Johor to prioritise people’s needs over mega projects such as JS‑SEZ and RTS
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim called on the Johor state government to address the people’s needs rather than focusing on the state’s mega projects. He said a state is not measured only by mega projects, but also the needs of the people. Anwar reminded that state governments that they have a responsibility to improve public housing, schools, mosques, roads and facilities in the villages and also in Felda schemes.

items.title: 10 teens arrested over assault on student in Tawau
items.summary: Tawau acting police chief Superintendent Wilynton Enchana Watt said the suspects, aged between 14 and 17, were arrested at 2pm yesterday. Wilynton said preliminary investigations showed the 14-year-old victim and three friends were taking a shortcut to the mosque along Jalan Sri Delima, Merotai Besar, when they were approached by a group of students. "The victim's three friends were also believed to have been assaulted by the group," he said in a statement.

items.title: RoS mulls mandatory audits, new mechanism to handle public donations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Registrar of Societies (RoS) is planning to introduce a new audit mechanism to tighten financial governance among registered societies, including non-governmental organisations (NGOs). Currently, societies are governed under the Societies Act 1966, which is undergoing review. Under Section 53 of the Societies Act, offences carry penalties of a fine not exceeding RM5,000 for registered societies.

items.title: #NSTviral: Sarawak pet shop accused of leaving cats out in the sun for 'sympathy buys'
items.summary: He and his wife said they noticed a cage containing Bengal cats placed outside the shop. However, they later discovered that one of the cats had died. "There used to be two Bengal cats.

items.title: JS-SEZ delay risks Johor's economic momentum, says exco
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Delaying the launch of the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) master plan any further could undermine Johor's economic momentum and investor confidence, Lee Ting Han said today. The caretaker Johor Investment, Trade, Consumer Affairs and Human Resources Committee chairman threw his support behind caretaker menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi's call for the immediate rollout of the blueprint, saying Johor could not afford to lose the momentum built over the past two years. "If Johor has achieved the nation's highest economic growth and investment even without the JS-SEZ master plan, imagine what more we can achieve once the entire JS-SEZ ecosystem is fully implemented," he said.

items.title: Former Umno stalwart Puad makes surprise appearance at Pakatan event, declares support for coalition
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 5 — Former Umno supreme council member Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi made a surprise appearance at a Pakatan Harapan (PH) youth event here last night. “My attendance here tonight was also to express my support for PH’s Puteri Wangsa candidate Maszlee Malik,” said the outspoken 68-year-old politician Earlier, Mohd Puad had openly declared his support with a campaign poster for PH’s Rengit candidate, Mohamad Yazid Bakri, for the Johor state election. In the 2022 Johor state election, Mohd Puad won the Rengit state seat with a 1,920-majority vote, defeating three other candidates from Perikatan Nasional (PN), Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR) and Parti Pejuang Tanah Air (Pejuang).

items.title: Venezuela quake death toll rises to nearly 3,000
items.summary: CARACAS: Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes killed nearly 3,000, updated official figures showed on Saturday, as international rescue teams began winding down search operations for survivors in the rubble of the disaster. Tens of thousands more are still reported missing. International disaster teams, including some US squads, and some South American teams were starting to finish up rescue operations, their members said on Saturday.

items.title: Mydin MD rejects perception of strong Bumiputera funding support
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Mydin Mohamed Holdings managing director Datuk Dr Ameer Ali Mydin has dismissed the perception that Bumiputera entrepreneurs enjoy extensive government support. He said meaningful financing was largely unavailable once businesses outgrow the micro-enterprise level. "People say the government provides financing.

items.title: US turns 250 with Trump center stage
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States celebrated its 250th birthday on Saturday with parades, flyovers and fireworks to come, as President Donald Trump stamped his mark on the landmark anniversary in a deeply divided nation. and "Trump! Trump!"

items.title: Johor polls: Anwar steps up PH campaign momentum, hits 8 state constituencies today
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today stepped up the coalition's campaign momentum for the Johor state election with a packed tour across eight constituencies in the northern part of the state, aimed at strengthening public support for its candidates and machinery. Today's tour will see the prime minister engaging with voters in Layang-Layang, Senggarang, Semerah, Bukit Naning, Pemanis, Gambir, Serom, and Palong Timur, which fall under the Buloh Kasap state legislative assembly seat, according to a post on his Facebook page. In the afternoon, Anwar is set to attend the 'Jelajah Harapan Felda Bersama PMX' programme at Dataran Putra Palong Timur 2 at 3.10pm, followed by a meet-and-greet session with residents of Pemanis at Kampung Jawa at 5pm.

items.title: Ringgit forecast to trade at RM4.05-RM4.07 against US dollar next week
items.summary: Bernama KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit is expected to trade between RM4.05 and RM4.07 against the US dollar next week, with market attention likely to centre on the United States Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) minutes and Bank Negara Malaysia's (BNM) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said traders, investors and economists would be watching the FOMC minutes closely for clues on the direction of US interest rates, especially given the absence of forward-looking guidance at the June FOMC meeting. Mohd Afzanizam added that the US dollar/ringgit pair may attempt to break the RM4.05 level next week.

items.title: North Korea's Kim oversees latest naval weapons tests
items.summary: SEOUL: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un oversaw weapons tests this week of the 5,000-tonne destroyer Kang Kon, including cruise missile launches and "electronic warfare means", state media reported Sunday. The tests took place on Friday, the state-run Korean Central News Agency (KCNA) said, less than two weeks after another 5,000-tonne destroyer, the Choe Hyon entered military service. During that commissioning ceremony, Kim vowed to equip his navy with nuclear weapons and develop new 10,000-tonne warships.

items.title: Johor election: Renggam not a 'dead town', say residents
items.summary: KLUANG: By dusk, many of Renggam's shopfronts already have their shutters pulled down, traffic begins to thin and many of the town's youths are kilometres away earning a living in Johor Baru, Singapore or Kuala Lumpur. To some, that is enough to label the Kluang district town "dead". Businessman Rahim Puari, 56, said it was misleading to call Renggam a dead town.

items.title: PM: Development in Johor needs to be balanced
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Johor needed development that was more balanced between rural areas, villages and the urban poor, as well as continued implementation of high-impact projects. He said the state's development should not only be focused on mega projects, but needed to be translated through the availability of basic amenities that directly benefit the people such as affordable housing, schools, stalls, community halls and other public facilities. "There is a big difference in terms of development between Johor Baru, Ulu Kempas, for instance Ulu Tebrau.

items.title: Egypt expects €1.5bil from EU assistance package in coming days, minister says
items.summary: DUBAI: Egypt expects to receive €1.5 billion (US$1.72 billion) from the European Union in the coming days, the first of two remaining tranches of a €5 billion macro-financial assistance package, Foreign Minister Badr Abdelatty said on Saturday. Speaking at a press conference in Egypt's new administrative capital alongside European Commissioner for the Mediterranean Dubravka Suica, Abdelatty said the outstanding €3 billion would be disbursed in two equal tranches of €1.5 billion each. The EU has so far disbursed €2 billion of the package, having transferred an initial €1 billion tranche in January 2025 and a second €1 billion earlier this year.

items.title: First-time voters urged to consider BN's track record
items.summary: Johor Umno deputy chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan said since independence, the BN government had brought prosperity and harmony to the nation. "The BN government has always brought development, progress, prosperity and stability to the people and nation. Earlier, Ahmad and BN Larkin candidate Mohd Hairi Mad Shah campaigned in Taman Sinaran Kempas.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia poised to extend uptrend, eyes 1,700 level next week
items.summary: On the index board, the FBM Emas Index jumped 91.99 points to 12,457.01, the FBMT 100 Index widened 81.71 points to 12,283.30, and the FBM Emas Shariah Index climbed 50.93 points to 12,292.23. Weekly turnover decreased to 13.63 billion units worth RM11.11 billion from 15.54 billion units worth RM13.11 billion a week earlier. The Main Market volume declined to 6.73 billion units valued at RM9.72 billion against 7.78 billion units valued at RM11.58 billion previously.

items.title: Johor polls: The choice between continuity and change
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: One week in, the Johor election campaign is shaping up to be a familiar choice between continuity and change, with Barisan Nasional maintaining a visible advantage as the July 11 polling day draws nearer. The campaign began on June 29. BN's messaging centres on state governance, economic development and leadership credibility, allowing the coalition to project administrative stability and continuity.

items.title: Main Washington July Fourth event evacuated over storm threat
items.summary: WASHINGTON: A massive July Fourth celebration on Washington's National Mall was evacuated on Saturday, due to an approaching severe thunderstorm, AFP reporters said. A few hours before President Donald Trump was due to deliver a campaign rally-style address to a huge crowd, fierce winds buffeted the grounds and lightning flashed nearby, prompting officials to order people to immediately seek shelter.--AFP © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Calls grow for national sex offender registry after Shah Alam case [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Calls are growing louder for Malaysia to establish a national sex offender registry and tighten safety measures at educational institutions following the arrest of a college employee in Shah Alam over the alleged sexual abuse of two 15-year-old boys. Parent groups said it was high time the government took their demands for a national sex offender registry seriously if they are concerned about protecting children from predators. "Protecting children must never be left to chance.

items.title: Subsidise community gyms where green spaces have disappeared, say residents
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Federal and state governments and local councils should subsidise community gyms in urban areas to offset the loss of green spaces and help tackle rising lifestyle-related diseases, said two residents groups. He said local authorities should work with gym operators to establish affordable community gyms in neighbourhoods and People's Housing Projects. Lee said community gyms were beneficial for residents seeking to remain active.

items.title: Gym group wants lower licensing in Selangor [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Gymnasium Entrepreneurs Association Malaysia has urged the Selangor government to follow Kuala Lumpur's lead by reducing annual licensing fees, saying the move would help make fitness more accessible. Its president K. Krishnakumar said community gyms — independently owned facilities commonly located in neighbourhood shoplots — primarily serve lower- and middle-income Malaysians. He said operators were seeking lower annual licensing fees in Selangor, where rates vary between local authorities.

items.title: Prayers to be offered for Iran's Khamenei on day two of funeral ceremonies
items.summary: TEHERAN: Prayers were to be said over the casket of slain Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei on Sunday, the second day of marathon funeral ceremonies that have already drawn huge crowds to pay their final respects in Teheran. Authorities have not announced whether a religious figure or a family member will lead the prayers for Khamenei, who ruled Iran from 1989 until he was killed aged 86 in an airstrike on the first day of the Middle East war on February 28. His son and successor Mojtaba Khamenei has not been seen in public since being named supreme leader, and is said to have been wounded in the same attack.

items.title: RMN adopts 'firm but measured' stance to safeguard maritime sovereignty
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Royal Malaysian Navy is taking a measured, non-aggressive military posture in asserting Malaysia's maritime sovereignty by working with other security agencies to address threats at sea. "In so-called grey-zone (non-military strategies involving subtle challenges) situations where enforcement responsibilities are shared, the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) takes the lead in enforcement action, while the navy provides support through maritime presence, deterrence and escalation management," he said. On regional maritime security cooperation, Zulhelmy said the navy was strengthening interoperability with strategic partners through exercises conducted domestically, regionally and internationally.

items.title: Putin, Zelensky speak to Trump ahead of NATO summit
items.summary: MOSCOW: US President Donald Trump spoke with his Russian and Ukrainian counterparts Vladimir Putin and Volodymyr Zelensky Saturday to discuss the conflict in Ukraine ahead of a NATO summit in Ankara, the Kremlin and Zelensky said. "The presidents naturally addressed the issue of a settlement in Ukraine, taking into account, in particular, Donald Trump's upcoming participation in the NATO summit in Turkey on July 7 and 8," said Yuri Ushakov, the Kremlin's foreign policy aide, according to the RIA Novosti news agency. He said the two leaders also discussed issues including Iran and the Middle East during the 85-minute call.

items.title: US VP Vance says Britain has been failed by leaders, hopes next PM delivers change
items.summary: LONDON: US Vice President JD Vance said Britain had been "failed by its leadership for a long time," adding that he hoped the country's next prime minister could deliver the structural change voters were seeking after years of political turmoil. In an interview with the Sunday Times newspaper, Vance said frequent changes of government pointed to deeper problems in British politics. Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced last month he would step down after two years in office, paving the way for Britain's seventh prime minister in a decade.

items.title: Ukraine attacks St Petersburg, says Russian seizure of key town a 'lie'
items.summary: UKRAINE: Ukraine denied Saturday Moscow's announcement that Russian forces had captured the strategic Ukrainian city of Kostyantynivka, a stronghold for Kyiv's troops in the eastern Donetsk region and a key target for the Kremlin. A Ukrainian army spokesman told AFP the city remained under its control, while President Volodymyr Zelensky dismissed Moscow's announcement as "a lie", with the duelling claims coming after Russia also said Ukrainian drones had struck its second city, St Petersburg. "The situation remains difficult," Ukrainian army spokesman Andriy Kovalyov told AFP, but Kostyantynivka "is under the control of the Ukrainian Defence Forces."

items.title: Tens of thousands rally in Albania against Trump-linked resort project
items.summary: ALBANIA: Tens of thousands rallied on Saturday in the Albanian capital Tirana in the largest protest since a movement against a resort linked to US President Donald Trump's family began. The 35th consecutive daily protest was the biggest since rallies started in late May against the planned construction of a luxury hotel linked to Trump's daughter Ivanka and her husband Jared Kushner in a nature reserve on the Balkan country's coast, AFP reporters said. Fifteen police officers were injured, while 25 demonstrators were detained, according to the police.

items.title: Ukraine's Zelenskiy says he spoke to Trump, calls for 'American resolve' to help end war
items.summary: UKRAINE: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy said on Saturday that he had spoken to US President Donald Trump on the Fourth of July holiday and called for "American resolve" to help end the more than four-year-old war with Russia. Zelenskiy said he had a "very good" conversation with Trump, discussing the situation on the front line. Zelenskiy said he and Trump had agreed to "continue our conversation" at next week's NATO summit in Turkiye.

items.title: Trump says Netanyahu 'knows who the boss is': Axios
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu could visit the White House as early as next week, US President Donald Trump told Axios on Saturday, adding that the prime minister "knows who the boss is" in their relationship. (Netanyahu) knows who the boss is," Trump said in a brief phone interview with Axios, referring to himself. "It might take place the week after," the official told Axios.

items.title: Man climbs Eiffel Tower, hangs US flag on America's big birthday
items.summary: PARIS: A man climbed the Eiffel Tower on Saturday and draped the US flag on Paris's emblematic landmark on the 250th anniversary of American independence, police said. The man was spotted in the afternoon climbing the tower between the second and third floors and hanging the Stars and Stripes. Seven million tourists pay to see the 324-metre (1,063-foot) tower each year.

items.title: Iran envoy says friendly nations to get 'special' Hormuz fee treatment
items.summary: BEIJING: Iran's ambassador to China insisted Saturday that new fees would be charged to ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz – an idea rejected by Washington – while assuring that "friendly" nations would receive special treatment. The initial deal struck between Iran and the United States to end their war stipulated that commercial ships would transit the strait free of charge for 60 days, but it remains unclear what will be in place after that period. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: In Johor, Anwar says politicians shouting ‘Long live Malays’ should walk the talk
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today criticised certain parties that frequently champion Malay supremacy, while at the same time, allowing Malay reserve land to fall into the hands of others. “Do not merely shout Bumiputera or Malay, long live the Malays, when elections are near, only to seize Malay contracts, Malay projects and Malay assets after gaining power. “I want to ask the parties that frequently speak about Malay supremacy, when was the last time you created Malay reserve land?

items.title: Teo Nie Ching calls for harmonious campaigning after Pakatan materials vandalised in several Johor seats
items.summary: KULAI, July 4 — Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching has urged all parties to campaign in a harmonious manner after incidents of vandalism involving Pakatan Harapan (PH) campaign materials were reported in several areas in the 16th Johor state election. “This has occurred, including under the Kulai parliamentary constituency, involving tents and campaign materials for the PH candidate for the Bukit Permai state seat, and was also reported in the Mengkibol and Kluang state constituencies, as well as several other areas,” she said. Yesterday, the PH candidate for Bukit Permai, Mohamad Shafwan Ani, claimed that his campaign materials in Bandar Putra had been covered using bunting belonging to another candidate.

items.title: Anwar mulls raising STR aid and rejects claims Malays’ rights are under threat
items.summary: KULAI, July 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said he agreed to increase the amount of Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR) cash aid in the future. “However, we will not allow people to steal the people’s money. Anwar, who is also the finance minister, reminded the people that they do not need to queue up for the federal government’s cash aid programmes.

items.title: Anwar questions ‘impatient’ dissolution of Johor assembly ahead of July 11 polls
items.summary: KULAI, July 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim took a veld swipe at caretake Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi today, questioning the early dissolution of the state assembly before the end of its mandated term. Without naming Onn Hafiz directly, Anwar described the state Barisan Nasional (BN) chief as "impatient" in calling for the snap poll, noting that elections are typically held every four to five years. The upcoming Johor state election will feature 172 candidates vying for 56 seats.

items.title: Economy Ministry: JS-SEZ master plan launch aligned with Malaysia-Singapore Leaders’ Retreat
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 4 — The launch of the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) Master Plan needs to be strategically coordinated to ensure policy alignment and a smooth implementation, while reinforcing investor confidence. The Ministry of Economy said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s decision to align the launch of the master plan with the Malaysia-Singapore Leaders’ Retreat in the fourth quarter of this year reflects the government’s strategy to secure bilateral commitment at the highest level, while enhancing global publicity. Meanwhile, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said JS-SEZ’s progress should be assessed based on tangible outcomes and economic impact.

items.title: Anwar defends Pakatan’s Johor push: ‘This is about helping the people’
items.summary: KULAI, July 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim emphasised that the visit by Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) top leadership to Johor is not intended to stir conflict, but rather to put forward policy measures designed to uplift the welfare of the state’s people. “This Johor state election is our way of helping the people. Has the Johor government not helped?

items.title: Two rallies in Kota Kinabalu split over corruption claims and support for CM Hajiji
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 4 — Two separate marches took place in the state capital today, with one led by student activists calling for greater accountability over corruption allegations in Sabah, while another gathered in support of Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor. About 50 people, mostly university students and civil society activists, took part in an anti-corruption march named Gempar Rasuah 3.0, organised by Suara Mahasiswa, while a separate pro-government gathering of about 100 participants moved along a different route through the city before ending at Kota Kinabalu City Park. Protesters carried banners, placards, loudspeakers and megaphones while chanting slogans including “Bangkit, bangkit” (rise up) and “tolak rasuah” (reject corruption), as well as singing songs denouncing alleged corruption among political leaders.

items.title: False 5:1 ratio allegation dismissed, UKM says only 3,917 international undergraduates among 46,151 students
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 4 — Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (UKM) has denied allegations circulating in a social media video claiming that the university has five international students for every local student. In a statement today, UKM described the claim of a 5:1 international-to-local student ratio as completely false. Based on the official figures, international undergraduates account for just eight per cent of the total student population, making the actual ratio approximately one international student for every 12 students - far from the alleged 5:1 ratio.

items.title: Food security drive: DPM Zahid vows 50pc import reduction by 2050, phased cuts already stabilising chicken prices
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, July 4 — The Ministry of Rural and Regional Development (KKDW) aims to reduce the country’s dependence on imported food by 50 per cent by 2050, Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said. The Deputy Prime Minister and Rural and Regional Development Minister said the target was set to address the country’s food import costs, which currently amount to about RM80 billion a year. “Currently, we import food worth about RM80 billion a year.

items.title: Malaysia keeps prawn ban: DOF says 30‑day assessment needed once Thai documents complete
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, July 4 — The Department of Fisheries (DOF) requires at least 30 days to assess documents submitted by the Thai authorities regarding the suspension of import permits (IPs) for five prawn species from that country, provided the information received is complete. “The information is currently being assessed by the technical team. If the information provided is complete, we will require at least 30 days to evaluate every document and piece of information submitted.

items.title: Entertainment tax ‘destroying accessibility’: Groups say Malaysians deserve affordable concerts, theme parks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 4 — Several organisations representing Malaysia’s entertainment, tourism and creative industries are calling for a review of the country’s entertainment tax and urging the government to develop a clear roadmap towards its total abolition. “Today, entertainment, tourism, theme parks, cultural and creative experiences are important contributors to economic growth, employment, tourism receipts, community well-being and national identity. Entertainment, tourism, culture and creative experiences are no longer luxuries.

items.title: PM Anwar declares war on support letters, says crony loans destroy agencies and entrepreneurs
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim stressed that the practice of using letters of support, political connections and cronyism in approving financing for entrepreneurs must be stopped. Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said the practice only harms government agencies and causes many businesses to fail. The Prime Minister said the government could not prevent business failures that occurred due to market factors, but would not tolerate the misuse of public funds.

items.title: Rembau police launch K9 hunt after reckless wrong‑way driver crashes, flees into palm oil plantation
items.summary: REMBAU, July 4 — Police are on the hunt for a man who fled after allegedly driving recklessly and against the flow of traffic before colliding with two other vehicles while trying to evade police arrest at Kilometre 242 of the North-South Expressway (NSE) northbound, early this morning. Acting Rembau district police chief DSP Azmi Ali said the incident occurred after the police ordered the 30-year-old driver to stop following a check that found the road tax and insurance for the Volkswagen Passat vehicle had expired. He said, however, the man did not stop and instead accelerated, driving dangerously in a zig-zag manner before going against the flow of traffic.

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain forecast in parts of eight states until 6pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 4 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in parts of eight states, with the weather expected to persist until 6pm today, according to Bernama. The national news agency reported that the warning covers Kota Setar, Yan, Pendang, Kuala Muda and Sik in Kedah; Manjung and Perak Tengah in Perak; and Kemaman in Terengganu. In Sarawak, the affected areas are Sibu in Sarikei division, and Tanjung Manis and Daro in Mukah division.

items.title: DBKL announces long-term road closures around Jalan U Thant from Monday
items.summary: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL), in a Facebook post, said the closure will affect the exit from Jalan U Thant to Jalan Tun Razak near Regal House, as well as the entry from Jalan Tun Razak to Jalan Langgak Golf near the Embassy of Iraq. According to DBKL, motorists travelling from Jalan Tun Razak to Jalan U Thant, Jalan Taman U Thant, Jalan Maidge, Persiaran Ampang Hilir, and Jalan Langgak Golf must use Jalan U Thant as the designated entry route. Meanwhile, motorists travelling from Jalan U Thant, Jalan Taman U Thant, Jalan Maidge, Persiaran Ampang Hilir, and Jalan Langgak Golf towards Jalan Tun Razak are required to exit via Jalan Langgak Golf.

items.title: Johor Jaya's Pakatan debutant bets on youth vote to shape state’s future
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 4 — Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) Johor Jaya candidate Lee Wern Yiing believes young voters will play a decisive role in shaping Johor’s future at the upcoming state election. Lee, who is also Johor DAP Socialist Youth (DAPSY) chief, is no stranger to the constituency, having spent the past eight years handling cases in Johor Jaya. The Johor Jaya state seat, located in Johor Bahru, is a growing constituency comprising residential neighbourhoods and light industrial areas.

items.title: Malaysia on track for 100pc internet coverage in populated areas by 2027
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, July 4 — The government through the Ministry of Communications is targeting 100 per cent Internet access in populated areas nationwide by next year through the implementation of the National Digital Network (JENDELA). Ministry of Communications secretary-general Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah said that as of Dec 31, broadband coverage in such areas had reached 99.71 per cent, which reflected the high commitment of the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC). He said that before the implementation of Jendela, internet access nationwide was only around 90 per cent, but through the latest phase of the plan, MCMC will focus on Internet access up to 4G and 5G.

items.title: Anwar says Bumiputera empowerment is national priority, not just agencies’ responsibility
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 4 — The Bumiputera empowerment agenda is no longer the sole responsibility of specific agencies but a national priority to be pursued by all ministries, government agencies and government-linked companies, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. He said government agencies must align their policies and programmes to ensure the Bumiputera development agenda is implemented comprehensively. According to Anwar, the government has introduced the Bumiputera Economic Transformation Plan 2035 (PuTERA35), with implementation regularly monitored and all ministries and agencies required to report their progress.

items.title: BN-Pakatan cooperation remains intact despite Johor campaign heat, says Zahid
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, July 4 — Barisan Nasional (BN) enjoys a very good relationship with Pakatan Harapan (PH) at the federal level, with cooperation between the two political coalitions remaining strong despite the heat of the Johor state election campaign. “Cooperation between BN and PH is very good. Commenting on the current Johor state election campaign, Ahmad Zahid said both political coalitions remain committed to preserving harmony within the Unity Government by adopting a mature approach to election campaigning.

items.title: Government’s RM429m allocation since 2023 ensures Johor’s enforcement agencies thrive
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 4 — The Home Ministry (KDN) has allocated more than RM429 million since 2023 to strengthen the welfare of personnel and enhance the operational capabilities of enforcement agencies in Johor. Its minister, Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, said the allocation covers three key agencies, namely the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), the Malaysian Immigration Department (JIM), and the Malaysian Prisons Department. Saifuddin Nasution said the allocation comprised RM174.8 million for projects that have been completed or are currently under implementation, and RM255 million for projects in the planning stage.

items.title: Puad Zarkashi declares support for Pakatan’s Rengit hopeful, says choice based on personality
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 4 — Former Umno supreme council member Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi has today declared his support for Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) Rengit candidate Mohamad Yazid Bakri for the Johor state election. The former Johor state assembly speaker and Rengit assemblyman had openly expressed his support for PH’s Mohamad Yazid through a campaign poster. In the 2022 Johor state election, Puad won the Rengit state seat with a 1,920 majority vote.

items.title: Mohamad Sabu leads Malaysian delegation in Tehran, pays final respects to slain Iranian leader Khamenei
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 4 — Minister of Agriculture and Food Security Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu led the Malaysian government delegation in paying final respects to the late former Supreme Leader of Iran, Ali Khamenei, at the Grand Mosalla in Tehran. In a Facebook post today, Mohamad said the mourning ceremonies would take place over several days, beginning in Tehran before continuing to Qom, Najaf, and Karbala, prior to the remains being taken to the Imam Reza Shrine in Mashhad for burial on July 9. “Alhamdulillah, the final respect for the late former Supreme Leader of Iran, Ali Khamenei, at the Grand Mosalla has just concluded.

items.title: Anwar says Johor needs smarter, bolder representatives, pledges Harapan will defend people’s interests
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 4 — The people of Johor need leaders and elected representatives who are wise in assessing priorities, have the courage to make decisions and truly understand the pulse of the people, said Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said the coalition would continue to carry the people’s voice, defend the state’s interests and drive Johor to become more developed and great for all. “Johor Progresses.

items.title: Penang DOSH issues prohibition notice after worker struck, killed by falling scaffolding platform
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 4 — The Department of Occupational Safety and Health (DOSH) Penang branch has issued a prohibition notice to halt work immediately at a Bandar Tanjong Pinang construction site following a fatal accident on July 1. In a statement, Penang DOSH said the order was issued as investigations are ongoing into the 1.30pm incident involving an Indonesian construction worker who was confirmed dead after being struck by a scaffolding working platform that fell from the eighth floor of a building. “Following the incident, a prohibition notice was issued to stop related work activities, along with a non-disturbance order to preserve the scene in its original condition until investigations are completed,” read the statement.

items.title: Amirudin dismisses claims Pakatan manifesto copied, says affordable housing and healthcare plans original
items.summary: KLUANG, July 4 — PKR vice-president Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari has dismissed claims that Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) manifesto for the 16th Johor State Election was copied from other parties, saying it was developed by the coalition’s leadership over several months. He said initiatives such as affordable housing and healthcare assistance were the result of careful planning by the PH leadership. Also present were PKR vice-president R. Ramanan, Amanah secretary-general Faiz Fadzil, PH candidate for the Machap state seat Nur Hafiz Roslan, PH Benut candidate Abd Razak Ismail and PH Layang-Layang candidate Guna Balakrishnan.

items.title: PM Anwar launches SParK 2026, PUNB sets RM2.25b financing target to drive Bumiputera growth
items.summary: The agency has announced a financing approval target of up to RM2.25 billion to strengthen the development of Bumiputera entrepreneurs for 2026–2030. Anwar said the national agency, established to develop Bumiputera entrepreneurs, has performed well thus far. It also introduced three programmes — Prosper Grow Biz Express, Prosper Grow Fuel Up, and Prosper Grow Auto Biz — to improve financing access and strengthen entrepreneurs’ working capital.

items.title: Bukit Aman flags 50pc jump in reckless driving cases, warns surge could trigger road rage accidents
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 4 — Cases of reckless and dangerous driving have increased by nearly 50 per cent in the first six months of this year compared to the same period last year. The increase reflects a worrying trend of aggressive driving, which could lead to road bullying or road rage incidents. Bukit Aman Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT) director Datuk Seri Muhammed Hasbullah Ali said 1,278 investigation papers were opened from Jan 1 to June 28 this year, compared to 852 cases in the same period last year, an increase of 426 cases.

items.title: College worker with prior child sex convictions arrested in Shah Alam after teenage boys allege assaults
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 4 — Police have arrested a man suspected of sexually assaulting two 15‑year‑old male college students in separate incidents here. According to Bernama, Shah Alam police chief ACP Sarudin Samah said the suspect, a local man in his 30s with a previous criminal record involving child sexual offences, was picked up at a restaurant in Shah Alam at 12.01am today. “The first victim alleged he was molested in the restroom of the college dormitory, while the second claimed he was sodomised at the suspect’s house in Section 18, Shah Alam,” Sarudin said in a statement to Bernama.
